title: DTR: Review hardcoded `if (true)` bypassing validator check
description: In `src/libs/network/endpointHandlers.ts`, the DTR relay logic has a `// REVIEW` comment with `if (true)` instead of `if (!isValidator)`. This means DTR relay runs for ALL nodes regardless of validator status - likely for testing. Need to evaluate if this should be conditional on actual validator status before production.
design:
## Location
`src/libs/network/endpointHandlers.ts:420-424`

```typescript
// REVIEW We add the transaction to the mempool
// DTR: Check if we should relay instead of storing locally (Production only)
// if (!isValidator) {
if (true) {  // Currently always runs DTR logic
```

## Decision Needed
1. Should DTR relay run for ALL nodes (current behavior)?
2. Should DTR relay ONLY run for non-validators (original intent)?
3. Are there edge cases where validators should also relay?

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decide on correct conditional logic
- [ ] Update `if (true)` to proper condition
- [ ] Test with both validator and non-validator nodes
- [ ] Remove `// REVIEW` comment once resolved
